title: HTML and XML content parsers
description:
Add HTML and XML content parsers behind optional cargo features, proving the projection is genuinely format-agnostic.

## Background

These parsers are implemented to force the content-parser trait to be format-neutral, but they are feature-gated so the default build stays lean. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` (step 10) and decision record section 6.2.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] HTML and XML parsers implement the `ContentParser` trait behind optional cargo features (off in the default build).
- [ ] [hard] A content rule (for example a section/item check) produces identical findings across Markdown, HTML, and XML inputs in a test with the features enabled.
- [ ] [hard] Default-build compilation and binary size are unaffected when the features are disabled.

## Notes

Anchors: `document/parser/` trait; `features` block in `crates/jit/Cargo.toml`.
